title: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
heading_path: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
text:
The Web Forms API is available in all developer accounts, but only in certain production account plans. Contact [Docusign Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US) or your account manager to find out whether the Web Forms API is available for your production account plan.

You can request more information from a recipient before they open and sign an envelope agreement using Web Forms. There are two options for presenting your recipients with your web form instances: embedded and remote.

title: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
heading_path: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
Learn about embedded web form instance delivery
text:
You can have your recipients view and sign web form instances within your app using *embedded web form instance delivery*. In embedded delivery, you create instances of your activated web form configurations for your recipients by embedding the web form instance URL in your app. Each recipient will get one web form instance which, for eSignature-based flows, will generate one envelope.

Embedded template-based web form delivery follows these steps:

1. Ensure that you have a web form configuration created from an existing eSignature template. The Web Forms builder extracts [document](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/concepts/documents/), [tab](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/concepts/tabs/), and [recipient](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/concepts/recipients/) information from the eSignature template to serve as the framework for your web form configuration, which you can edit as needed. This template can include multiple recipients.
2. Call the [Instances:createInstance](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/reference/webforms/instances/createinstance/) API endpoint, using your web form configuration ID as the call’s form ID. You can find your web form’s configuration ID by calling the [Configurations:listForms](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/reference/webforms/configurations/listforms/) endpoint and finding the form entry in that call’s response.
3. The generated web form instance will include an instance URL, which you can open in your app. This displays the web form, and the recipient is prompted to enter information for the web form fields assigned to them.
4. After they finish entering their information in their web form, the recipient is presented with their envelope, which is prefilled with their web form instance information. They are then prompted to fill in any envelope tabs assigned to them and/or sign the agreement.

If there are multiple recipients, only the primary recipient is presented with the web form instance in an embedded view. Additional recipients access the web form instance via an envelope email.

See [Web form instance processing](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/web-forms-101/instance-processing/) for details and flow diagrams.

title: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
heading_path: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
When to use embedded web form instance delivery
text:
Embedded web form instance delivery has two main advantages:

- Because you are embedding the web form instance into your app, your user is already logged in and authenticated. This offers the highest level of security.
- Embedding the form instance directly in your app provides a seamless experience where your users can view and sign forms immediately.

Use embedded web form instance delivery when your users need to complete and sign forms instantly, directly within your digital environment, or when you need to support high-volume, public, or in-person workflows without relying on email distribution.

title: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
heading_path: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
When to use embedded web form instance delivery
text:
- **Self-service workflows**
  - **Account registration:** New users can fill out and sign onboarding forms immediately after creating an account, without waiting for an email.
  - **Service requests:** Customers can submit signed service requests or support forms directly from your portal.
- **Walk-in or in-person scenarios**
  - **In-branch or in-office signing:** Banks, clinics, or government offices can have clients complete forms on tablets or kiosks during their visit.
  - **Event check-in:** Attendees can sign waivers or registration forms on-site at events or conferences.
- **Immediate action required**
  - **Time-sensitive agreements:** Users can complete forms instantly (e.g., urgent consent forms, last-minute registrations) without email delays.
  - **Real-time transactions:** E-commerce or financial platforms can capture signatures as part of the checkout or transaction process.

Embedded web form instance delivery also supports standalone web form configurations, which do not have a template and do not generate an envelope. These forms are used purely for data collection.

title: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
heading_path: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
Learn about remote web form instance delivery
text:
You can send web form instances directly to one or more recipients via email using *remote web form instance delivery*. These recipients each fill out their own view of a web form instance, and the information from those form instances is used to prefill and personalize an eSignature envelope that each recipient will then complete. 

You can use this feature to send web form instances to your recipients through the UI or by making a call to the [Instances:createInstance](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/reference/webforms/instances/createinstance/) API endpoint. Remote web form instance delivery follows these steps:

1. Ensure that you have a web form configuration created from an existing eSignature template. The Web Forms builder extracts [document](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/concepts/documents/), [tab](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/concepts/tabs/), and [recipient](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/concepts/recipients/) information from the eSignature template to serve as the framework for your web form configuration, which you can edit as needed. This configuration can include multiple recipients. Older versions of a web form configuration may not support remote delivery; in these cases, recreate the form.
2. Call the [Instances:createInstance](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/reference/webforms/instances/createinstance/) API endpoint, using your web form configuration ID as the call’s form ID. To indicate that you are using remote web form delivery, you must also include the following body fields:
   - `"sendOption": "now"`
   - A `recipients` object or a `formValues` object containing required values for each recipient. See [Required user values for remote web form instances](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/plan-integration/deliver-remote-instances-to-users/#required-user-values-for-remote-web-form-instances) for details.
   You can find your web form’s configuration ID by calling the [Configurations:listForms](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/reference/webforms/configurations/listforms/) endpoint and finding the form entry in that call’s response. Any recipients whose information is not provided in the request body will have their assigned tabs removed from the generated web form instance and envelope.

   See [How to create and send a web form instance](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/how-to/create-send-instance/) for a detailed walkthrough.
3. In routing order, each recipient receives an email containing a link to an envelope. This envelope will include an embedded web form instance if your web form configuration specifies that they must fill out fields for that web form instance. If they select the link, their browser opens the web form instance and they are prompted to enter information for the form fields assigned to them. Users who are  [not required to fill out the web form instance](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/plan-integration/identify-instance-users/) will be routed directly to the envelope.
4. After they finish entering their information in the web form instance, the recipient is presented with the envelope, which is prefilled with web form instance information. They are then prompted to fill in any envelope tabs assigned to them and/or sign the agreement.

See [Deliver remote web form instances to users](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/plan-integration/deliver-remote-instances-to-users/) for details on how to generate remote web form instances, or [Web form instance processing](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/web-forms-101/instance-processing/) for more information on the flow and diagrams.

title: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
heading_path: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
Learn about remote web form instance delivery
Restrictions for using remote web form instance delivery
text:
- Standalone web form configurations are not supported.
- Attachment fields are not supported.
- Older form configurations may not be compatible with the new remote sending functionality. To check for compatibility, you should look at the `allowSending` flag under `formProperties` (which requires a [Configurations:getForm](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/reference/webforms/configurations/getform/) API call).

title: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
heading_path: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
When to use remote web form instance delivery
text: Use remote web form instance delivery for flows that do not require an embedded experience. This enables you to rely on Docusign to handle the user flow from end-to-end.

title: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
heading_path: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
When to use remote web form instance delivery
text:
- **Mass onboarding**
  - **Employee onboarding:** HR departments can send onboarding forms (e.g., tax forms, direct deposit, policy acknowledgments) to new hires.
  - **Vendor/supplier onboarding:** Procurement teams can distribute registration or compliance forms to vendors.
- **Customer agreements and consents**
  - **Service agreements:** Companies can send updated terms of service or consent forms to customers who need to review and sign.
  - **Policy updates:** When privacy policies or terms change, organizations can request acknowledgment from affected users.
- **Compliance and regulatory requirements**
  - **Annual compliance certifications:** Financial institutions or healthcare providers can send compliance attestation forms to employees or clients.
  - **Audit documentation:** Collect required documentation from multiple parties for regulatory audits.

title: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
heading_path: When to use embedded or remote web form instance delivery
text:
- Learn more about [Web Forms concepts](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/web-forms-101/concepts/).
- Review the steps to [Deliver remote web form instances to users](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/plan-integration/deliver-remote-instances-to-users/).
- Get the details about [Web form instance processing](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/web-forms-101/instance-processing/).

title: Configure Web Form Fields
text:
Learn how to add and configure fields in a Web Form template using the Web Form Builder.

You can import template fields, such as "First name" and "Last name," and add new fields like "Middle Name" as needed. For information about each field type property, see the [Web Forms Field Type Properties](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=con145288b7-86ef-44fb-ab44-252ece5dec25.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Web Forms Builder to configure field types and their properties, including shared options such as envelope custom fields (ECFs) and sender controls.") guide.

1. Select the plus sign (+) at the top or bottom of the field where you want it to display.
2. Select the field type (Text, Number, Date, Email, and other types) that you want to add.

   The field displays in the center panel of the Web Form Builder.
3. Select the Properties or Rules tab to configure field properties or rules as needed.
4. Connect the field to a template field. For more information about connecting fields, see the [Connect Web Form Fields to Template Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=vlb1680643208628.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

The field is added to the form, connected to the appropriate template field, and its properties are configured according to your selections.

title: The Signature Page
text:
Read about the Signature page in the Web Forms Builder to review recipient information.

You can use the Signature page to review the primary recipient and additional recipient information associated with a form.

![Signature page in the Web Forms Builder with options to initiate signing from email, enable document field editing, and configure template and recipient connections.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gmi1660583110357/page/images/wf-signature-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTIxMjUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ21pMTY2MDU4MzExMDM1NyJ9.V9wwT8wwOTSGOaBVhjL1xqMOMkdee8WwoTbF8KV1KIw&_LANG=enus "Signature page in the Web Forms Builder with options to initiate signing from email, enable document field editing, and configure template and recipient connections.")

Initiate signing session from email
:   Toggle to initiate a signing session from an email link. This action verifies the signer's email.

    After you activate the form, select Open Link > Start, and add the signing information. Select Next to display the Summary page.

    The recipient selects Review Document in the email to display the signing page. If you don't toggle this, the signing page automatically displays without going through the email flow.

Enable document field editing
:   Toggle to allow recipients to edit envelope responses before signing, enabling modifications to all fields populated from the form.

    Once the form is activated, recipients can review and modify field values before signing. Typically, template fields remain unchanged, but this toggle allows updates, such as date fields. If you don't toggle this, the field editing is disabled.

Template
:   Select Edit (pencil) to view the template.

Template Connections
:   Configure the connection between form fields and template fields. For more information about how to connect the fields, see the [Connect Web Form Fields to Template Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=vlb1680643208628.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

Recipient Connections
:   Activate recipients and assign them form fields to collect their information.

title: Edit Recipient Information
text:
Learn how to review and edit recipient information on your Web Form to ensure accuracy and completeness.

You can review and edit recipient information on your form to ensure it is accurate and complete.

Note: You can update these fields, but you cannot delete them or leave them blank.

1. Select Templates > My Web Forms.
2. Select the form for which you want to edit the recipient information.
3. Select the Signature page from the
   Outline.
4. In the Template Configuration section, select the pencil icon next to Recipient Connections.
5. Select Edit Template.
6. From the Fields recipient dropdown, select Edit Recipients.
7. Edit recipient information or add a new recipient by selecting Add Recipient.
8. Select Save and Save and Close to apply the edits to your form.

Recipient information on the form is updated, and all changes are saved and applied to the selected template.

title: Web Forms
text: Read about how Web Forms simplifies creating and completing digital forms online, streamlines data collection, and generates signed agreements.

title: Web Forms
text:
Web Forms are online documents based on an eSignature template, an uploaded file, or a standalone document that recipients complete to generate a signed agreement, reducing manual data entry and improving their experience.

Note: FedRAMP accounts have access to Web Forms.

title: Web Forms
text:
Web Forms streamline data collection for your organization by simplifying how information is captured and managed.

You can:

- Build and edit interactive forms with a no-code interface to reduce IT costs.
- Allow recipients to complete forms easily on mobile devices.
- Enable basic form edits, such as adding or configuring fields.
- Use conditional logic to show or hide fields based on recipient input.
- Pre-populate a PDF agreement with Web Form data and send it for signature.
- Scale and support more advanced use cases with [Advanced Web Forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=cew1729885982169.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Web Forms
text:
Before creating a form, make sure you know how to create and use eSignature templates:

- If you are new to eSignature templates, see the [Template Basics](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=oxv1578456598913.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
- Have a template ready? Review the template checklist in the [Prepare a Template to Import to a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=xtj1660594293452.html&_LANG=enus) guide to confirm it is compatible with the form.

title: Get a Web Form URL
text:
Learn how to get the URL for a Web Form so you can email it to recipients or embed it on your website.

You can email a form URL to recipients or embed it on your website. You get the URL when you activate the form or select it from an activated form.

1. [Activate a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=iri1669070601520.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select Use at the top of the form.
3. In the Get URL tab, select the copy link.
4. Select Open Link  to view the form.

The form URL is copied to your clipboard and can be shared with recipients or embedded on your website.

title: Web Form Field Type Properties
text:
Learn how to use the Web Forms Builder to configure field types and their properties, including shared options such as envelope custom fields (ECFs) and sender controls.

You can use Web Forms Builder field properties to control field behavior, appearance for senders and recipients, and whether values are stored for reuse across envelopes.

To make a field an envelope custom field (ECF), toggle the Save as envelope custom field option. For more information about ECFs, see the [Envelope Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=ywu1578456351241.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

For each field type, you can use the Sender controls to set field visibility to Hide, Display (Optional), or Required.

| Field type | Properties |
| --- | --- |
| Text | Configure the text field name and description, choose the text type and character limit, optionally add a default value, and apply response validation. |
| Email | Configure the email field name and description, optionally add a default email address, and control sender visibility. |
| Date | Configure the date field name and description, choose a date format and default value, and optionally save it as an envelope custom field (ECF). |
| Radio | Configure the radio field name and description, and edit the radio options using the Edit (pencil) icon. |
| Checkbox | Configure the checkbox field name and description, define checkbox options (including option text and descriptions), choose a response validation option and default value, and control sender visibility. |

title: Sending a Web Form
text:
Learn how to send Web Forms directly from the Docusign web app to specific recipients, streamlining processes with sender-initiated forms and customizable sender controls.

You can start a web form directly from the Docusign web app and send it to specific recipients by email. This shift from public self-service URLs to sender-initiated forms streamlines processes that require a sender to prefill information or target specific recipients.

title: Sending a Web Form
text:
Unlike public URL web forms, where the envelope is created only after the recipient submits the form,
sender-initiated web forms generate a Docusign envelope immediately once the sender
selects Send.

The workflow for a sender-initiated form is:

1. **Initiation:** A sender logged into Docusign selects an active web form and chooses the Send option.
2. **Pre-fill:** The sender enters the recipient's information (name and email) and can optionally
   complete specific fields designated for the sender.
3. **Delivery:** Recipients receive a standard Docusign envelope email.
4. **Form completion:** When recipients select the email link, they go to the web form to review the sender’s information, complete any remaining fields, and then proceed to the standard signing ceremony.

   ![Animated example of a sender selecting an active web form in Docusign, entering recipient details, and sending the form for completion and signing.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gmi1660583110357/page/images/wf-use-form.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTIyNTEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ21pMTY2MDU4MzExMDM1NyJ9.U1bvfjH9zdzbKWYzoAuVPJPCiEvDRYhnWMrNZQfr4PQ&_LANG=enus "Animated example of a sender selecting an active web form in Docusign, entering recipient details, and sending the form for completion and signing.")

title: Sending a Web Form
text:
Process builders can customize the sender's experience through specific configurations:

- **Sender view:** A Data Pre-fill Page in the Web Forms Builder collects sender-only data for internal use (such as envelope custom fields) and is hidden from recipients.
- **Sender controls:** Process builders use sender controls to decide whether senders can pre-fill each field. Fields can be set to Hide (default), Display (optional), or Required for the sender. Sender controls are available in the Properties tab for each field in the Web Forms Builder.

![Sender view of the Web Form showing the recipient selection dropdown and related options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gmi1660583110357/page/images/wf-recipient-dropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTIyNTEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ21pMTY2MDU4MzExMDM1NyJ9.U1bvfjH9zdzbKWYzoAuVPJPCiEvDRYhnWMrNZQfr4PQ&_LANG=enus "Sender view of the Web Form showing the recipient selection dropdown and related options.")

title: Sending a Web Form
text:
- **Licensing:** Basic sending with the Docusign web app is available for standard Web Forms.
  Advanced capabilities, such as multi-recipient support or sending with
  the API, require the Advanced Web Forms add-on for eSignature plans but
  are included in IAM plans.
- **Unsupported fields:** Web forms with Attachment fields do not currently support sending.

title: Reactivate a Web Form
text:
Learn how to reactivate an edited Web Form so that your changes replace the currently active version and become visible to recipients.

After editing an active form, you must reactivate it for your changes to become visible and actionable to recipients.

1. Open an activated form and select Edit Form.
2. Make the necessary edits and select Activate.

   Note: When you reactivate an edited form, it replaces the current active form.
3. Select Activate and Replace.

   The form reactivates and is displayed.

The edited form is reactivated and replaces the previously active form.

title: Troubleshooting Web Form Template Error Messages
heading_path: Troubleshooting Web Form Template Error Messages
text:
Read about how to resolve common template error messages when importing templates into Web Forms.

You can resolve common template error messages that occur when you import templates into Web Forms by reviewing the specific message and applying the recommended fix.

The template must have at least one document
:   - A template must contain at least one document.
    - Make sure you add at least one document to the template.

The template must not have supplemental documents because your account is not fully enabled for this feature
:   - Your current eSignature plan isn't enabled to add supplemental documentation.
    - Remove the supplemental documents from the template. Upgrade to an eSignature plan supporting supplemental documents to add this feature.
:   Note: This error usually occurs when you move a form configuration from an account that supports supplemental documents to one that doesn’t support supplemental documents.

    For more information about supplemental documents, see the [Supplemental Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=zsq1583277359502.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

All recipients on the template must have a unique role name
:   - One (or more) of your recipients on the template doesn't have a unique name.
    - Make sure that all recipients on the template have a unique role name, for example, Applicant, Co-applicant, Signer, and so on.

The template must not include "In Person Signer", "Signs with Witness", or "Signs with Notary" recipients
:   - These recipient roles are currently not supported in Web Forms.
    - Remove any "In Person Signer", "Signs with Witness", or "Signs with Notary" recipients from the template.

The template must have a "Needs to Sign" recipient in the first position of the signing order
:   - There's no recipient listed in the first position of the signing order.
    - Make sure that at least one recipient in the first position of the signing order is assigned to a "Needs to Sign" action.

The primary recipient on the template must not be a signing group
:   - The primary recipient cannot be a signing group.
    - Make sure that the recipient who is designated as the "primary recipient" on the form isn't a signing group.

**Recipients can only have "email" as a delivery option**
:   - The SMS delivery is not currently supported.
    - Make sure all recipients on the template only have email as a delivery option.

The template must not use SMS or phone authentication
:   - SMS and phone authentication are not supported
    - Make sure that identity verification settings associated with recipients on the template are not using SMS or phone authentication.

Placeholder recipients on the template must have blank values for both name and email
:   - The name and email fields for a placeholder recipient are not blank.
    - Make sure that placeholder recipients on the template have blank values for both the name and email fields. If you are referencing a signing group, ensure it's properly configured as a recipient on the template. For more information about signing groups, see the [Signing Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=zgn1578456447934.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

      Note: This error usually occurs when a form configuration is moved from an account that has a specific signing group to one that doesn't.

The template must not use SMS or phone authentication
:   - SMS and phone authentication are currently not supported in Web Forms.
    - Ensure that the identity verification settings associated with recipients on the template are not using SMS or phone authentication.

To continue set an envelope type
:   When you select the "Edit Template Settings" action in Web Forms, you must assign an [envelope category](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=tvp1724803837658.html&_LANG=enus) to the template. This field is required.

title: Troubleshooting Web Form Template Error Messages
heading_path: Troubleshooting Web Form Template Error Messages
text:
Forms, you must assign an [envelope category](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=tvp1724803837658.html&_LANG=enus) to the template. This field is required.

Data label can't be blank
:   - A data label on your template was left blank.
    - A data label identifies a data field. You cannot leave a data label blank; you must add a descriptive name in the Data Label field.

**Data label must have a descriptive display name**
:   - The data label name isn't descriptive. It might be a machine code (for example, "45&ar\*00") or blank.
    - Add a descriptive display name for the data label on the template in the Data Label field. A descriptive name reflects the field. For example, a label for a Name field might be "Applicant Name".

Dropdown options can't be blank
:   - The dropdown field options descriptions are blank.
    - Add unique values in the dropdown Options field on the template.

Dropdown options must be unique
:   - There are duplicate dropdown field options. All options must be unique.
    - Ensure that all dropdown options are unique. Change any duplicate option on the template.

Group Label can't be blank
:   - The Radio Group label is blank.
    - Add a descriptive group label for a Radio Group field

Group Label must be descriptive
:   - Group data label must be descriptive.
    - Add a descriptive display name for the group label on the radio fields.

Radio values can't be blank
:   - The radio values are blank on the template.
    - Make sure you add unique radio values.

Radio values must be unique
:   - There are duplicate option values, which must be unique.
    - Make sure that all radio values are unique.

Checkbox values can't be blank
:   - The checkbox values are blank on the template.
    - Add a unique name for each checkbox.

Checkbox values must be unique
:   - There are duplicate checkbox values on the template.
    - Make sure the checkbox value that you add is unique

Checkbox data labels must be unique
:   - The data label on the template is not unique.
    - Make sure the checkbox data label you add is unique.

    Select the checkbox group and enter a name in the Data Label field. In this example, the data label is Locations. Then select the Checkbox Values field and add values that match the number of checkboxes. In this example, the values are "North", "South", "East", and "West".

    For more information about using data labels, see the [Create a Data Label on a Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=yis1665092718418.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how to create clear, descriptive data labels on template fields so senders can easily identify each form field.") guide.

The template must not include any pre-fill document fields.
:   - Pre-fill document fields are not supported in Web Forms or in Workflow Builder workflows that use Web Forms.
    - Remove all pre-fill document fields from the template before importing it into Web Forms or using it in a Workflow Builder workflow.

title: Differences Between Web Forms and PowerForms
heading_path: Differences Between Web Forms and PowerForms
text:
Read about how Web Forms and PowerForms are both template-based solutions for accessing, completing, and signing forms, each offering unique features and benefits.

Web Forms and PowerForms are both template-based solutions that help users to access, complete, and sign forms. However, there are some differences between Web Forms and PowerForms.

title: Differences Between Web Forms and PowerForms
heading_path: Differences Between Web Forms and PowerForms
text: Web Forms provide an interactive, guided experience for completing forms and improve envelope completion rates by 31% compared to PowerForms, offering a superior signing experience despite requiring more setup.

title: Differences Between Web Forms and PowerForms
heading_path: Differences Between Web Forms and PowerForms
text:
A PowerForm is a static PDF form that users can complete. It allows self-service PDF forms to be published on websites without requiring coding. For more information about PowerForms, see the [PowerForms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jbx1643062255110&topicId=eke1578456649038.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

| Feature | PowerForms | Web Forms |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Generates envelopes? | The envelope is triggered when the recipient *begins* completing the form. | The envelope is triggered when the recipient *completes* the form.  Web Forms never waste an envelope because it is created *only when signing begins*. |
| Mobile-friendly user interface? | Recipients complete information on a PDF that may not be optimized for mobile devices. | Recipients complete the information on a dedicated form that is mobile-friendly. |
| Conditional logic? | You can set conditional logic in templates to hide or show fields on a PowerForm. | You can set conditional logic to hide or show fields. |
| Supports multiple users? | Multiple users can complete and sign a PowerForm. | Web Forms supports multiple users. For more information about multi-recipient Web Forms, see the [Build a Multiple-Recipient Web Form﻿](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=inc1745950777430.html&_LANG=enus) guide. |
| Shareable unique link? | Yes | Yes |
| Branded UI? | Yes | Yes |

title: Differences Between Web Forms and PowerForms
heading_path: Differences Between Web Forms and PowerForms
Convert a PowerForm to a Web Form
text:
You can convert a PowerForm to a Web Form in two ways:

- Select Create Web Form from a PowerForm.
  - On the Agreements page, select PowerForms.
  - In the PowerForms list, find the form to convert.
  - Select More Actions (...) > Create Web Form.
- Select the template that you already use for a PowerForm and create a Web Form from it.
  - The PowerForm still exists in your account. For information about building a Web Form, see the [Build a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=euk1665093155272.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Copy an Existing Web Form for a Workflow Builder Workflow
heading_path: Copy an Existing Web Form for a Workflow Builder Workflow
text:
Learn how to copy an existing Web Form as a standalone form for use in a Docusign Workflow Builder workflow.

You can copy an existing form for a Docusign Workflow Builder workflow. Use the Create Workflow Version option to extract the signature functionality from the original form and create a standalone form.

Note: To copy a form for Docusign Workflow Builder, select Create Workflow Version, not the Copy option.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select My Web
   Forms, Shared with Me, or All
   Web Forms.
3. Select the form to use in the Workflow Builder workflow.
4. Select More Actions (...) > Create Workflow Version.

   The form is copied and named "Workflow Copy - [Web Form name]", for example, "Workflow Copy - Residential Loan Application" for the "Residential Loan Application" form.

The workflow copy is created as a standalone form with the original signature functionality preserved for use in your Workflow Builder workflow.

title: Build a Web Form
text:
Learn how to build a Web Form by choosing a starting point, customizing the fields and layout, and activating the form so it is ready to share.

You can build a form by choosing a starting point, customizing the fields and layout, and then activating the form so it is ready to share with recipients.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select Start > Web Forms > Create Web Form.
3. Choose how you want to start:
   1. Select an eSignature template: Select an existing eSignature template from your library to start the Template-to-Form flow, or choose an industry-standard template with Web Forms enabled from the Docusign Web Forms Library by selecting the Choose from pre-built form library link.
   2. Create blank form: Build a standalone form to collect data and download the results, or use a standalone form with Docusign [Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=gua1698120920620.html&_LANG=enus) workflows.
   3. Upload document: Select a document from your device to start the document-to-form flow.
4. Review and edit the imported fields as needed.

   - [Configure Web Form Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=awi1670975283111.html&_LANG=enus).
   - [Add Rules to a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=kff1668121038223.html&_LANG=enus).
5. When you finish building the form, [activate it](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=iri1669070601520.html&_LANG=enus).

Your form is ready to email or embed on your website.

title: Download the Web Form CSV Results
heading_path: Download the Web Form CSV Results
text:
Learn how to download a CSV file of submitted Web Form data to review, filter, analyze, or integrate the results with other systems.

You can download a CSV file of all submitted form data to analyze it or integrate it with external systems.

Note: The CSV results may take several hours, up to 24 hours, to display after the form is completed.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select My Web Forms, Shared with Me, or All Web Forms and choose the form whose CSV results you want to download.
3. Select More Actions (...) > Download Results.

The CSV file downloads to your device so you can review, filter, and analyze all submitted form data.

title: The Web Forms Builder
text:
Read about the Web Forms Builder, which lets you create and configure Web Forms from templates or from scratch, customize fields and attributes, and apply conditional rules to control which fields display.

A form inherits its attributes and data labels from the template used in the Web Forms Builder. While forms may vary in appearance due to different templates, they share common building characteristics and navigation.

Standalone forms that are not based on a template can also be created and configured with their own attributes and data labels.

Each form begins with a Welcome page, where you can modify the title, subtitle, and button text.

![Web Forms Builder Welcome page showing the Outline panel on the left, form preview in the center, and Properties and Rules on the right.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gmi1660583110357/page/images/wf-welcome-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTIxMTEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ21pMTY2MDU4MzExMDM1NyJ9.PCobyyOEGhMmm1YG0B0dRwrpCNUXA4EHk-Cqdt_h588&_LANG=enus "Web Forms Builder Welcome page showing the Outline panel on the left, form preview in the center, and Properties and Rules on the right.")

Outline
:   Displays on the left side of the builder. This panel contains the form fields inherited from the data labels in the template. Select the fields and they display in the center panel of the builder.

Center
:   Displays the form fields. When you select a field from the Outline, it displays in the center panel. This panel reflects the final form.

    For more information about adding fields to a form, see the [Configure Web Form Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=awi1670975283111.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

Properties/Rules
:   Displays on the right side of the builder. You can add or edit the properties of the form fields. For example, you can edit a field name, or add the selections to a dropdown or checkbox field.

    You can also create rules that show other fields, depending on the recipient's response. For example, you can set a rule that if a recipient responds "Yes" to the field "Are you married"? then the field "Spouse's name" displays.

    For more information about rules, see the [Add Rules to a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=kff1668121038223.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

More Actions (...)
:   - Edit Template Settings: Open and edit the template.

      Note: When you use this menu action to edit a template, make sure that you set the [envelope categories](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=tvp1724803837658.html&_LANG=enus) attribute on the template. This is a required field.
    - Edit Template Fields: Edit the template fields.
    - Edit Template Connections: Review and edit template connections.
    - Edit Recipient Connections: Activate recipients and assign fields to collect information.
    - Manage Data Access: Manage users and groups with access to the web form.
    - Rename: Rename the form.
    - Share: Share access with a user or group on the account. For more information about sharing access, see the [Manage Access to Web Form Submission Data](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=kbg1740597699270.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
    - Download Configuration: Download the JSON file.
    - Delete Form: Delete the form.

title: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
text:
Read about the limitations and considerations for Web Forms, including analytics, attachments, customization, data management, and more.

There are some limitations and considerations for Web Forms:

title: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
text:
Does Web Forms have an analytics or reporting feature?
:   Yes. For more information, see the [Generate a Field-Level Data Report to Analyze Web Form Responses](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=ynu1689886125053.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
text:
How many attachment fields can you add to a Web Form?
:   You can add up to 15 attachment fields to a form.

title: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
text:
Can you customize a Web Form?
:   Yes. You can apply a logo and update branding colors on a form. For more information, see the [Add a Brand to a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=zpa1664994114237.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

Can you present data in several structures, for example, a credit card number in the structure XXX-XXX-XX?
:   Custom text field validation is supported with regular expressions, which require data to match a specific format (such as XXX-XXX-XX). For more information, visit the [Regex website](https://regexlearn.com/).

Can you use formula fields in a Web Form?
:   Formula fields are not currently supported in Web Forms but they are supported during signing.

Can you embed a Web Form in an iframe?
:   Yes, but be aware of the limitations and concerns about using iframes. For more information, see the [Better alternatives to iframes for embedding documents](https://www.docusign.com/blog/developers/the-problems-iframes) article.

title: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
text:
Can you write a script for a conditional rule, or are the conditions built-in?
:   There is no scripting functionality when specifying conditional rules.

title: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
text:
Is there a version management process for Web Forms?
:   Web Forms provides basic version management, allowing you to edit a Draft version without affecting the Active version. When you activate a draft, it becomes the active version. You cannot revert to a previous version.

Can you reuse Web Forms data?
:   Web Forms collects data for a single use, such as completing a document. To reuse data across multiple forms or documents, use Web Forms APIs to automatically populate forms with previously captured data. For information about APIs, see the [Web Forms API 101](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/) guide.

title: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
text:
Is Web Forms mobile responsive?
:   Yes. Web Forms is mobile responsive.

title: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
text:
Do you need an internet connection to complete a Web Form?
:   A Web Form requires an internet connection.

title: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
text:
Can you limit the number of form submissions (similar to the PowerForms functionality)?
:   No, this functionality is not currently available.

Is it possible to have multiple users complete the same form?
:   Yes. Web Forms supports multiple users. For more information about multi-recipient Web Forms, see the [Build a Multiple-Recipient Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=inc1745950777430.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

Can you change the eSignature “sender” for envelopes generated from a Web Form?
:   Yes, but you can't assign a sender directly. When you activate a Web Form, an authorization step requests permission for a user to be the eSignature sender for envelopes generated from that form.
:   The user who authorizes the account and activates the form does not need to be the original creator or owner, but must be an eSignature administrator. To change the sender, an eSignature administrator reactivates the form, authorizes their account, and activates the form on behalf of the creator or owner.

Can you use "In Person Signers", "Sign with Notary", or "Sign with Witness" recipient signer types for a Web Form?
:   No, currently Web Forms does not support these signing types.

title: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
text:
Can you associate a Web Form with multiple templates?
:   Currently, a Web Form can be associated with only one eSignature template.

Can you pass variables and values into the Web Form URL as you can with a PowerForm link?
:   Yes. For more information, see the [Prefill a Web Form By URL](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=kup1721242003741.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
heading_path: Limitations and Considerations for Web Forms
Web Forms and other eSignature features
text:
Will Web Forms replace PowerForms?
:   No. You can continue to use PowerForms and convert an existing PowerForm to a Web Form. For more information, see the [Differences Between Web Forms and PowerForms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=dly1660584518825.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

Is Advanced Web Forms included in Web Forms?
:   No. Advanced Web Forms is a separate add-on to Business Pro or higher Docusign eSignature direct plans and is included in Docusign IAM plans (Professional and higher).

Can you use a document generation template in Web Forms?
:   Web Forms does not support document generation. Remove any documents that use document generation from templates that include this functionality.

Can you use Web Forms with Bulk Sends?
:   Not at this time.

Can you use Web Forms with CLM?
:   Web Forms are created and managed in eSignature and can trigger CLM workflows through Agreement Actions. However, you can't bypass the signature step after submitting the form, so this might not be the ideal experience.

Does Web Forms support CFR Part 11 accounts?
:   Yes. Select "Initiate signing session from email" in the Web Form signature step.

Can you resend an envelope generated with Web Forms?
:   Yes, but only when "Initiate signing session from email" is enabled in the Web Form configuration (on the signature page in the Web Forms Builder).

Can you use collaborative fields on templates associated with Web Forms?
:   Yes. If you select "Changes Require Initials", you must also select "Initiate signing session from email" in the Web Form configuration (on the signature page in the Web Forms Builder). For more information about collaborative fields, see the [Collaborative Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=lsv1579552570897.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Deactivate a Web Form
text:
Learn how to deactivate a Web Form so it can no longer be viewed or shared.

You can deactivate an active form to prevent it from being viewed or shared.

1. Select My Web Forms, Shared with Me, or All Web Forms and open the form that you want to deactivate.
2. Select More Actions (...) > Deactivate.

   The Deactivate Form? window displays.
3. Select Deactivate.

The form is deactivated and can no longer be viewed or shared. To reactivate a form, see the [Reactivate a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=crq1669076320407.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Populate Read-Only Fields on a Web Form
heading_path: Populate Read-Only Fields on a Web Form
text:
Learn how to populate Web Forms with read-only fields by setting default values or using the Web Forms API so recipients cannot change prefilled information.

Sometimes you need to create a form with read-only fields. For example, you might have a radio field with "Yes" or "No" options that should always be "Yes" for certain users, or a form where only one recipient’s information is prefilled.

You can create a form with prefilled values that recipients cannot change or delete. Add values for read-only fields in two ways:

- **Set default values for the field.** Select the default value for the field that you want to make read only. For example, for a dropdown field named "Locations", select "PNW" as the default value and toggle the Read only option.

  Note: Use this for fields with default options, such as options or checkboxes.
- **Set values through the Web Forms API.** Use this method for fields without default values, such as "Name" or "Email". For example, for a form for a specific person, use the Web Forms API to prefill personal information fields, such as "Applicant Name", so they are read only. For more information about the Web Forms API, see the [Web Forms API 101](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/web-forms-101/) guide.

  Note: Read-only fields can't be modified by the person completing the form. Their values must be set either through the API or by assigning a default value.

title: Make Web Form Fields Read-Only
text:
Learn how to make a Web Form field read-only for recipients.

You can make a form field with default values read-only so recipients can view but not change the information.

1. Build or edit a form.

   Note: For fields without default values, such as a Name field, see the [Prefill a Web Form By URL](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=kup1721242003741.html&_LANG=enus) guide and the [Web Forms API 101](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/web-forms-101/) guide.
2. Select the field you want to make read-only.
3. Toggle the Read only option in the Properties tab.

The form recipient can see the Read only field but cannot edit its value.

title: Build a Multiple-Recipient Web Form
text:
Learn how to create a multiple-recipient Web Form from a multi-signer template so each signer sees and completes only their assigned fields.

By default, a form created from a multi-recipient template is completed only by the first recipient. Multi-recipient forms let additional recipients from the template complete the same form.

Create a multiple-recipient form from a multi-signer template so each signer sees only their assigned fields.

For example, in a two-signer template, the first signer completes and signs the form, which then emails the second signer to complete their fields. Each recipient sees only their fields, but they share the same envelope.

Keep these points in mind when building a multiple-recipient form:

- Attachment fields are not supported for additional recipients.
- The additional recipients don't see the Thank You page from the
  form configuration.
- You can add or remove recipients after you create the form. From the Outline, select the recipient dropdown and Manage Recipients.
- If you remove a recipient, their form fields are permanently deleted, and any customizations are lost.

Note: Multiple-recipient forms are available only for Advanced Web Forms. You can create multiple-recipient forms only from templates. You cannot create them as standalone forms.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select Start > Web Forms > Create Web Form.
3. Select Start with document > Select an eSign template.
4. Choose a template with multiple signers, and select Next or Continue without AI.
5. From the dropdown, select the form initiator, and select Next.
6. Select the recipients who must complete the form, and select Create Form.

The form is created with the signers that you added, each with their own fields. Each recipient receives an email to complete their fields, and when all are finished, the form is ready to review or download.

title: Add a Field Rule to a Web Form
heading_path: Add a Field Rule to a Web Form
text:
Learn how to use the Web Forms Builder to create, edit, and apply show-or-hide rules to form fields and pages.

You can create rules easily using the Web Forms rule builder to show or hide specific fields and pages based on how users respond to form questions.

Note: Form users can't change read-only field values. An administrator updates these values using an API or by setting a default value for the field.

In the IF section:

- Select the field that you want to add a rule to. That field displays after
  IF.
- Select an operation for the field. An operation is an action. For example, "is" or "is not",
  "filled in" or "not filled in", and so on. Depending on the operation, you may
  see an additional field. For example, if you select the operation "is on or
  after", a date field displays.
- When you select an operation, a plus sign (+) displays so you can add another condition.

In the THEN section:

- Select Show or Hide to show or
  hide a field or page.
- For the Select field, page, or document field, select the field, page, or document for the rule to show or hide.

Note:

- When you create a web form from a template, its rules are automatically imported.
- If you update or add rules in the template, they don't automatically sync to the form. Likewise, rule changes on the form don't sync back to the template after the initial import, so you must manually update or add rules on the form.

1. Select the field on the form where you want to add a rule.
2. Select the Rules tab. If there are rules associated with the field, they display on the Rules tab.
   If no rules are associated with the field, the message "No rules applied to
   [field name]" displays in the Rules tab.
3. Select Create Rule. The name of the field you selected displays in the IF
   field, for example, "Date of Birth".
4. For IF, select the dropdown to choose an operator.

   An operator is a word or phrase that defines the rule, such as is, is not, contains, or does not contain.

   A new field displays.
5. Add a value to the blank field. For example, for the "Date of Birth" field, you
   selected the is on or after operator. Enter the date in
   the date field.
6. To add another field to the rule, select the plus sign (+). For example, if you want the response from a second field, select the and or or operator, and then select the field.

   After setting up the IF, you must set up the
   THEN part of the rule.
7. Select Show to display a field based on a response, or Hide to hide a field.
8. Select the field to display when the rule conditions are met. For example, for a rule on the Date of Birth field, select "Provide valid identification".
9. After the rule is set up, select Apply.

   The following example shows the rule, "If the birthday is on or after Jan. 1, 2005, then
   show Provide valid identification".

   The rule displays on the Rules tab.
10. Select the pencil icon to edit the rule, if necessary.

After you add or edit rules, the form dynamically shows or hides fields and pages based on user responses, and the configured rules display on the Rules tab for the selected field.

title: Activate a Web Form
text:
Learn how to activate a Web Form, authorize your account, set access, and make the form available to recipients.

After building the form, you must activate it to make it visible and usable for recipients.

1. Select Activate.
2. Select Authorize Account to verify your account as the authorized sender of the form.

   ![Authorize Account confirmation message indicating the account must be authorized before activating the Web Form](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gmi1660583110357/page/images/wf-activate-first-time-message.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTIyMjYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ21pMTY2MDU4MzExMDM1NyJ9.ZuEmSmTr0vxe0E_bsAHGqsZjTxOo8dxUIcxtUpMNa48&_LANG=enus "Authorize Account confirmation message indicating the account must be authorized before activating the Web Form")

   Important: If the account used to authorize the form is closed, envelope creation fails. To fix this, a user with form access (such as an account administrator) must reactivate and reauthorize the form using an active account.
3. Choose an access setting for the form; you can change it later.

   ![Access settings dropdown for activating a Web Form with Public and Private options](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gmi1660583110357/page/images/wf-activate-this-web-form.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTIyMjYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ21pMTY2MDU4MzExMDM1NyJ9.ZuEmSmTr0vxe0E_bsAHGqsZjTxOo8dxUIcxtUpMNa48&_LANG=enus "Access settings dropdown for activating a Web Form with Public and Private options")

   1. Select Public to make the form visible to anyone through a shareable link.

      Important: The form must be activated as "Public" to be connected to a Workflow Builder workflow.
   1. Select Private to make the form available only through API
      integration.
4. Select Activate.

   The form activates and is displayed.

The form is activated and available to recipients based on the selected access setting.

title: Format Text in Page Subtitle and Field Description Fields
heading_path: Format Text in Page Subtitle and Field Description Fields
text:
Learn how to use Markdown to format plain text in the Page subtitle and Description text fields, including bold, italics, headings, lists, links, and horizontal lines.

You can format plain text in the Page subtitle or Description text fields by using Markdown to apply formatting.

Note: Markdown adds formatting to plain text. For more information, see the [Markdown](https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/) website.

- [Bold](https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/#bold)
- [Italic](https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/#italic)
- [Links](https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/#links)
- [Headings](https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/#headings)
- [Lists](https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/#lists-1)
- [Special characters](https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/#characters-you-can-escape)
- [Horizontal lines](https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/#horizontal-rules)

For more information about configuring fields, see the [Configure Web Form Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=awi1670975283111.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Manage Access to Web Form Submission Data
heading_path: Manage Access to Web Form Submission Data
text:
Learn how to manage access to form submission data by sharing it with users or groups and controlling their permissions.

Form creators and administrators can manage submission data with other users or groups on an account. This data includes all the information from the collected responses, such as recipient names.

A user can have access to all form submission data or no access:

- Can Access All Data: Users can access all form submission data.
- Remove: Access is removed from the user.

Note: The user you share form data with must have Web Forms permissions. For information about assigning Web Forms permissions, see the [Add Web Form Permissions for Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=kfl1679340366644.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select My Web Forms or Shared with Me.
3. Select More Actions (...) next to the form that you want and select Manage Data Access.

   The Manage data access for this web form window opens.
4. In the Add user or group field, enter the names of the users or groups that you want to grant data access to.
5. Select Can Access All Data from the dropdown.

   Note: If you want to remove access from the user or group, select Remove from the dropdown and Save.
6. Select Share.

The user or group you added now has access to the form submission data.

title: Build a Workflow Builder -Compatible Web Form
heading_path: Build a Workflow Builder-Compatible Web Form
text:
Read about how to create a Workflow Builder-compatible Web Form to automate the agreement process without coding.

The Docusign Workflow Builder platform integrates workflow steps for Docusign applications like Web Forms, automating the agreement process end-to-end without coding.

For more information about using Workflow Builder, see the [Workflow Builder Overview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

You can build a Workflow Builder-compatible form in two ways:

- [Build a Standalone Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=pbx1716585514741.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Copy an Existing Web Form for a Workflow Builder Workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=edj1716586721977.html&_LANG=enus)

title: Add Rules to a Web Form
heading_path: Add Rules to a Web Form
text:
Read about how conditional and document rules control which fields and documents display in a Web Form based on recipient responses.

Conditional rules control which fields display on a form based on recipient responses. They target specific information by showing additional fields when certain conditions are met.

You can use conditional rules to show or hide fields, pages, or sections based on conditions, and import rules from a template or create them directly on the form for any content type.

You create rules using an "if...then" structure. For example, if the response to "Do you take medication"? is "Yes", show the "List your medications" field. If the response to "Are you married"? is "No", hide the "Spouse" field.

For more information about adding field rules, see the [Add a Field Rule to a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=inf1660603502823.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

You can also set up document rules for forms. Document rules control additional documents that only some recipients must review. For more information about adding document rules, see the [Add a Document Rule to a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=qvz1729637084872.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Add Web Form Permissions for Users
heading_path: Add Web Form Permissions for Users
text:
Read about how administrators can assign and manage Web Forms permissions for non-administrators using permission profiles or support tickets.

Administrators automatically have Web Forms access. Non-administrators need assigned permissions. If the Admin tab is missing in Web Forms, an administrator must assign your permissions profile. For information about template and form permissions, see the [Permission Profile Options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mfo1583277360424.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

Administrators and form creators can share form access with non-administrators, who can view, edit, or activate a shared form based on their assigned access level.

title: Add Web Form Permissions for Users
heading_path: Add Web Form Permissions for Users
text: Administrators must first grant users template permissions. This establishes the baseline permission level for that user. If your account allows separate form permissions, administrators must also grant a form permission. To manage web-form-only permissions separately from template permissions, you can [submit a support ticket](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/How-Do-I-Open-a-Case-in-the-DocuSign-Support-Center).

title: Add Web Form Permissions for Users
heading_path: Add Web Form Permissions for Users
text: If necessary, administrators must grant user-specific form permissions. This level can match or be more restrictive than the template permission, but not more permissive.

title: Add Web Form Permissions for Users
heading_path: Add Web Form Permissions for Users
text:
The specific meanings of the different permission levels (none, use, create, share) are the same for both template permissions and form permissions.

- **None:** Users can't access any forms or templates.
- **Use:** Users can access shared forms and templates, but can't edit them.
- **Create** or **Share:** Users can create and edit forms or templates.

Administrators can assign permissions through two main methods:

1. **Permission profiles:** This is the recommended method. Permission profiles allow you to manage permissions for groups of users efficiently.
2. **Support tickets:** This method is used when you must manage form permissions separately from template permissions. This is less common but can be useful in specific situations.

   Note: Forms permissions can't exceed templates permissions. For example, you can't add a form **Create** permission to a user who has the template **Use** permission. If the form selection you want is not activated, check that the template permission level is sufficient.

title: Prefill a Web Form By URL
heading_path: Prefill a Web Form By URL
text:
Learn how to prefill Web Form fields by adding API-based values to the form URL so recipients see partially or fully completed forms. Prefilling by API is available only with Advanced Web Forms.

Manual form prefilling works like [PowerForms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jbx1643062255110&topicId=pnz1578456644088.html&_LANG=enus): when you send the form link, recipients see some or all fields already filled in.

With Advanced Web Forms, you can prefill a form using an API. For more information, see the [Web Forms API 101](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/web-forms-101/?_gl=1*mhe2jk*_gcl_au*MTc1NTM0NTY1NS4xNzczOTUyMTI4) guide.

Prefilled values use the API reference name in the Properties tab. You can edit this name to match the field. Prefill values override default values.

Tip: This feature is intended for users comfortable working with code. Decide which fields to prefill before you start. Prefill values override default values.

Text field
:   The value can be any valid text. Percent-encode special characters and spaces. For example, to prefill a text field with "New York", enter `New%20York`. For more about percent-encoding (URL-encoding), see the [Percent-encoding](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Percent-encoding) guide.

Email field
:   The value can be any valid, properly formatted email address. Web Forms validates that the value is a properly formatted email on the web form.

Date field
:   The value must use the YYYY-MM-DD format.

Number field
:   The value must be in the format XXXX.XX. Use periods for decimal places and exclude "thousands" separators. For example, don’t use numbers such as 10,000 or 20.5. Instead, use 10000 or 20.5.

Dropdown field
:   The value must match a valid API value from the dropdown options.

Radio field
:   The value must match a valid API value for a radio option.

Checkbox field
:   The value must match a valid API value from the checkbox options. To select multiple options, use a comma-separated list of API values, for example, "yes", "no", "maybe".

1. Open an active form. For more information about activating a form, see the [Activate a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=iri1669070601520.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
2. Select Use.
3. In the Get URL tab, select the copy link icon.
4. Select Edit Form.
5. Select the field that you want to prefill, and in Properties, find API reference name.
6. Copy the API reference name.
7. Enter a hashtag (#) at the end of the URL link.
8. Enter the [API reference name]=[Value]. For example, https://demo.services.docusign.net/webforms-ux/v1.0/forms/5ce56ce725a431d3b8fe47ffd761e963#Applicant\_name=Mary%20Jones.
9. To add another field, enter an ampersand (&) and the field's API reference name.

   https://demo.services.docusign.net/webforms-ux/v1.0/forms/5ce56ce725a431d3b8fe47ffd761e963#Applicant\_name=Mary%20Jones&Primary\_Phone=123-234-2345.
10. To prefill a dropdown or radio field, use its API reference name and API value. For example, to select beige for a field named "My Color", use the value "Beige".

    https://demo.services.docusign.net/webforms-ux/v1.0/forms/5ce56ce725a431d3b8fe47ffd761e963#Applicant\_name=Mary%20Jones&Primary\_Phone=123-234-2345&My\_Color=Beige.
11. To add a checkbox field, enter a comma-separated list of values for the field's API reference name. For example, for the "My\_ShirtStyle" checkbox, enter the value for each option you want to select.

    https://demo.services.docusign.net/webforms-ux/v1.0/forms/5ce56ce725a431d3b8fe47ffd761e963#Applicant\_name=Mary%20Jones&Primary\_Phone=123-234-2345&My\_Color=Beige&My\_ShirtStyle=Long%20Sleeve,Short%20Sleeve,Button%20Down.

When someone opens the URL with prefilled values, the form fields are already completed, helping ensure accurate and efficient submission.

title: The Hidden Data Page
text:
Learn how to use the Hidden Data Page in Advanced Web Forms to capture and manage envelope custom field values, including populating them with default values or by the Web Forms API.

You can capture data visible only to you on the Hidden Data Page. When you create a form from a template with envelope custom fields (ECFs), they are imported automatically.

To verify that a field is an ECF, select it and the Save as envelope
custom field toggle displays in the Properties tab.

There are two ways to add values to fields on the Hidden Data Page:

- Set the Default value in the Properties tab.
- Use Web Forms APIs to automatically populate fields. For more information about Web Forms APIs, see the [Web Forms API 101](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/web-forms-101/) guide.

  Note: The Hidden Data Page is an Advanced Web Forms feature for Docusign eSignature plans and is included with Docusign IAM Professional and higher plans. Advanced Web Forms is an add-on for Business Pro and higher plans.

title: Save Envelope Custom Fields (ECFs)
text:
Read about how to use envelope custom fields (ECFs) to categorize envelopes, save form data, and customize envelope email subject lines.

Envelope custom fields (ECFs) help you categorize and identify envelopes you send for signature. You can save form data as ECFs and use it to search and report on envelopes or customize the envelope email subject line. For more information about customizing the subject line, see the [Use Merge Fields in the Template Email Subject to Customize Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=bat1578456393880.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

If you are new to envelope custom fields (ECFs), see the [Envelope Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=ywu1578456351241.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

Note: When you reference a form field as a merge field, you must use the Field name property, not the API reference name property.

When you create a Web Form from an eSignature template, all ECFs from the template are automatically imported to the form's sender view. For more information about the sender view, see the [Sending a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=con56dbba74-0e1a-4356-a265-bf7ec87913e9.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

You can also designate additional form fields (except checkboxes) as ECFs by selecting the Save as envelope custom field option in the Properties tab.

title: Web Forms Accessibility
text:
Read about how the Web Forms Recipient Experience complies with Level A and Level AA criteria of the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.1.

Based on the most recent assessment, the form-filling user experience of Web Forms, referenced in the following document as the “Docusign Web Forms Recipient Experience” supports all relevant Level A and Level AA criteria of the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.1. For more information about Web Forms accessibility, see the [Docusign Web Forms - Accessibility Conformance Report](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=docusign-web-forms-accessibility-conformance-report&topicId=docusign-web-forms-accessibility-conformance-report.pdf&_LANG=enus).

title: Change the eSignature Sender
text:
Learn how to change the eSignature sender for envelopes generated from a web form by authorizing a new sender.

Although you can't assign a sender directly, you can change the eSignature sender for envelopes generated from a web form by authorizing a different user.

When a web form is activated, the user who completes the Authorize My Account step becomes the eSignature sender, even if they didn't create or own the form.

Important: If the current sender's account is deactivated or they revoke Web Forms access, envelope generation for that form fails.

1. As the form manager, open an active web form and select Edit Form.
2. Select More Actions (...) > Share to give a new user Can Edit or Can Manage access.

   Important: The new sender must authorize their account and activate the form.
3. As the new sender, select Activate to finish activation.
4. Select Authorize Account.

   If Authorize Account isn't visible, select Edit next to Authorize eSignature sender.
5. Select Activate and Replace.

Future envelopes generated from the form are sent by the new eSignature sender.

title: Web Form Copy Options
text:
Learn how to copy a published Web Form by using the Copy and Create Workflow Version options.

After you publish your form, you can either create an exact copy of the form or generate a workflow-only version without the eSignature step.

Copy
:   This option creates a duplicate of the form, labeled with "Copy" before the title. To copy a form, select More Actions (...) next to the form that you want to copy, and select Copy.

Create Workflow Version
:   This option copies an existing form, removes the eSignature step, and labels the copy with "Workflow Copy" before the title. Use this option for Docusign Workflow Builder forms, which support only forms without a signature step. To create a workflow version, select More Actions (...) next to the form, and select Create Workflow Version.

title: Importing Attachment Fields from an eSignature Template
heading_path: Importing Attachment Fields from an eSignature Template
text:
Learn how to import attachment fields from an eSignature template into a Web Form and add additional attachment fields manually as needed.

When you create a form from a template that includes attachment fields, those fields are imported automatically. Because each attachment field accepts only one file, you may need to add more attachment fields.

For example, if you import a template with an attachment field named "Lease Agreement", users can upload one lease agreement. If users have multiple leases, you can manually add another attachment field, such as "Lease Agreement 2", for the second lease.

You can also add attachment fields directly to a form, even if the imported template has none.

Note: You can add up to 15 attachment fields to a form.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select Start > Web Forms > Create Web Form.
3. Select Start with document > Select an eSign template.
4. Select a template with multiple signers and select Next or Continue without AI.
5. If you select a template with attachments, select Import from the Import attachment
   fields? window.
6. If you select a template without attachments, select Got it from the Web Forms
   template copy created window.
7. Name the form and select
   Apply.
8. To add one or more attachment fields manually, select the plus sign (+).
9. Select Attachment.
10. Activate the form and send it to recipients so they can upload attachments.

The form is created from the selected eSignature template, and any attachment fields in the template are imported. Any attachment fields that you add manually are also available for recipients to upload files.

title: Build a Standalone Web Form
text:
Learn how to build a standalone Web Form to collect data for use in Docusign Workflow Builder workflows.

Build a standalone form to collect data and use it in Docusign Workflow Builder workflows. Standalone forms are not associated with a template and do not support signatures.

When you create the standalone form, it includes a Welcome page, an untitled page for collecting information, and a Thank you page.

With Advanced Web Forms, you can use APIs to populate standalone forms. For more information, see the [Web Forms API 101](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/web-forms-api/web-forms-101/) guide.

Note: Data verification is supported only for standalone forms and only for email and phone fields.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select Start > Web Forms > Create Web Form.
3. Select Create blank form.
4. Name the form and select Apply.
5. Build the form by adding fields, rules, and other elements. For more information about building forms, see the [Build a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=euk1665093155272.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

The standalone form is created with a Welcome page, an information collection page, and a Thank you page, and is ready to use in Docusign Workflow Builder workflows.

title: Diagnose the "Form Not Submitted" Error
text:
Learn about using a debug code in the form URL to diagnose the "Form not submitted" error.
