If you see a "Form not submitted" error after submitting a form, it is caused by an envelope generation error, which can result from the eSignature template configuration or account settings.

1. Add `?debug=1` to the end of the form URL.

   A notification displays the API error that caused the form submission to fail.
2. Use the error message to do one of the following:

   - Resolve the issue described in the error message. For example, if the message says, "The Tab formula is not valid", review and correct the Tab formula.
   - If you can't resolve the error, contact [Docusign Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/contactSupport) and include the error details.

After you resolve the issue or contact [Docusign Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/contactSupport) with the error details, the form submits successfully.

title: Add a Document Rule to a Web Form
heading_path: Add a Document Rule to a Web Form
text:
Learn how to add document rules to a Web Form template so specific documents display only to recipients who meet defined conditions.

You can add rules to any document in a form template so it displays only to specific recipients when the rule conditions are met. The template must include at least two documents.

For example, your organization sends a New Account application to residents of New York, California, and Texas. New York residents must complete an addendum, so you add it to the template and create a rule that requires only New York recipients to review and sign it.

Note: This functionality is available only in Advanced Web Forms, which is sold as a separate add-on to Docusign eSignature plans and is included in Docusign IAM plans (Professional and above).

1. Add the document to the template.
   1. Add the document when you create the form, or
   2. Select Template Actions in the Web Forms Builder, and select Edit Template Settings to add the document.
2. In the Web Form Builder, select the Rules tab to set up the rule.

   Rules you create for fields on additional documents do not automatically apply to the form. Add the rule manually in the form.
3. Select the field for the rule.

   For example, for a document for New York residents only to sign, you might select the State field.
4. Create a rule for the selected field as described in the [Add Field Rules to a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=inf1660603502823.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
   1. Specify the condition (IF) for the selected field.
   2. Specify the action (THEN) for the rule, and select the document you want to show under Documents.

      For example, to set up the rule for the New York document, select "New York Addendum".
5. To add more rules, select Create Another Rule as needed, and activate the form.

   When you set up a rule for a field on the additional document, the rule doesn't apply to the form automatically. Add the rule manually on the form.
6. Activate the form.

When recipients open the form, only the documents whose rule conditions are met are displayed for them to review and sign.

title: Share Access to a Web Form Configuration
heading_path: Share Access to a Web Form Configuration
text:
Learn how to share access to a Web Form by assigning view, edit, manage, or remove permissions to users and groups on your account.

You can share access to forms with users and groups on an account.

Before you begin, confirm that you have permission to manage forms and know which form and access level (view, edit, or manage) to assign.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select My Web Forms, Shared with Me, or All Web Forms.
3. Select More Actions (...) next to the form that you want to share and select Share.

   The Share this web form window opens.
4. In the Add user or group field, enter the names of the individuals or groups that you want to share the form with.
5. Select the type of access that you want to grant:

   - Can View: Get the URL for an activated form by selecting Get URL next to the form on the Web Forms list.
   - Can Edit: Edit and activate the form.
   - Can Manage: Manage form access and data. Creating forms also requires a separate permission managed by account administrators.
   - Remove: Revoke access to the form.
6. Select Save.

The user access settings are updated. Users and groups you selected now have the specified level of access to the form.

title: Open and Edit a Web Form
heading_path: Open and Edit a Web Form
text:
Learn how to open and edit an existing Web Form in the builder, and save and activate your changes.

You can open an existing form from the Templates page to update its fields, layout, or settings.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select My Web Forms, Shared with Me, or  All Web Forms.

   Only administrators can access forms from All Web Forms.
3. Select the web form that you want to open.

   The form opens in the builder.
4. If you are editing an activated form, select Edit Form.
5. Select Save.
6. [Reactivate the form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=crq1669076320407.html&_LANG=enus) so that the edits are visible to your users.

The form opens in the Builder.

title: Send a Web Form
text:
Learn how to send a Web Form, including prerequisites and step-by-step instructions for
sending the web form to recipients.

title: Send a Web Form
text:
- Have an active form that you have created or has been shared with you.
- The form must be linked to an envelope template.
- The form must not contain attachment fields.
- To send to multiple recipients, your account must have Advanced Web Forms or an IAM
  plan.

You can send a form that you created or that an administrator or form creator shared with you directly to recipients.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select My Web Forms, Shared with Me, or  All Web Forms.
3. Choose the form that you want to send and select Use.
4. In the Use Web Form window, select the
   Send tab.
5. Enter the name and email for the recipients.
6. Select Next.
7. In Pre-fill Form Details, complete the fields assigned to you.

   Note: Fields marked with a red asterisk are required and
   must be completed before you can send the form.
8. Review the information and select Send Web Form.

The system generates an envelope immediately and sends a notification email to the
recipients. You can track the progress on the Agreements page
under All Agreements.

title: Configure Dynamic Recipients
text:
Read about how to configure dynamic recipients in a Web Form, making certain roles optional to streamline the envelope generation process.

When importing a template with a Role field that is not always needed, you can make it optional so it is included only when necessary.

For example, if your template includes "Applicant" and "Co-Applicant" roles, but the co-applicant is not always required to sign, you can configure the template to include that role only when needed.

The primary recipient completes the form and enters their own and the additional recipient's name and email, which are used when the envelope is generated.

In this example, the Co-Applicant is optional. The primary recipient enters their name and email, leaves the Co-Applicant fields blank, and those fields are excluded when the envelope is generated.

To make a recipient optional:

- Select the recipient field (for example, the Co-Applicant), and clear the Required option.
- Activate the form. For more information, see the [Activate a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=iri1669070601520.html&_LANG=enus) and [Reactivate a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=crq1669076320407.html&_LANG=enus) guides.
- The recipient field displays on the form without the Required star.
- The primary recipient enters their name and email in the Primary Recipient field and leaves the Additional Recipient field blank. The additional recipient role is excluded when the envelope is generated.

You can hide the co-applicant fields with conditional rules, such as hiding them when a primary recipient is present, so they are excluded when generating the envelope. For more information about adding rules, see the [Add Rules to a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=kff1668121038223.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Pause and Resume a Web Form
heading_path: Pause and Resume a Web Form
text:
Learn how to allow form recipients to pause and resume completing a Web Form using the Finish Later button, or require the form to be completed in a single session by turning off this option.

Form recipients can pause and resume a form by selecting the Finish Later button, which saves their progress and emails a reminder link valid for 10 days.

The Finish Later button displays when the form is activated and sent. To require completion in a single session, add `?showFinishLater=false` to the end of the form URL.

Note: The Finish Later button is not available for [standalone forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=pbx1716585514741.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Open the form link.
2. Select Finish Later.
3. Enter an email address and select Submit.

   A 10-day valid link to resume the form is emailed.
4. Open the emailed link and select Finish Form to resume.

The recipient can pause and resume the form without losing previously entered data. If the Finish Later option is turned off using `?showFinishLater=false`, the form must be completed in a single session.

title: What Recipients See on a Web Form
heading_path: What Recipients See on a Web Form
text:
Read about how recipients start, complete, review, and submit a Web Form.

After you build and activate a form, you can share its URL by email or as a link on your website. Recipients open the link and select Start to complete the form.

For example, a new bank account creation form may display as:

![The first page of the Web Form where the recipient enters their personal and account information.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gmi1660583110357/page/images/wf-welcome-page1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTIyNzMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ21pMTY2MDU4MzExMDM1NyJ9.EqakeXfkfpRS6QGeLf5CFm59iHEzZwyaBxTFwrE1sxw&_LANG=enus "The first page of the Web Form where the recipient enters their personal and account information.")

After completing the form, recipients review their responses on the Summary page. To edit a response, they select Back and go to the fields they want to change.

![The Summary page of a completed Web Form displaying all recipient responses for final review before submission.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gmi1660583110357/page/images/wf-summary-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTIyNzMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ21pMTY2MDU4MzExMDM1NyJ9.EqakeXfkfpRS6QGeLf5CFm59iHEzZwyaBxTFwrE1sxw&_LANG=enus "The Summary page of a completed Web Form displaying all recipient responses for final review before submission.")

Recipients select Submit and are taken to the agreement to sign.

title: The Thank You Page
text:
Read about how to customize the Thank you page that confirms form submission, including modifying titles, subtitles, and adding buttons.

After submitting a Web Form, recipients see a Thank you page confirming receipt. You can use the default page or customize it.

![Example Thank You page showing a confirmation message and optional button after form submission](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gmi1660583110357/page/images/wf-thank-you-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTIxMjgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ21pMTY2MDU4MzExMDM1NyJ9.7sqm_7HaeaN8FbT9rLYQCqiUoYnUo6eM7Xa7-oO6WRA&_LANG=enus "Example Thank You page showing a confirmation message and optional button after form submission")

Page title
:   The default page title is "Thank you", which can be changed. This field must not be empty.

Page subtitle
:   A subtitle displayed under the page title. The default is "We've received your form", but you can change it.

Button
:   You can add a button by switching this toggle. The default is no button. If you add a button, provide the Button text and Button URL. These fields cannot be blank.

title: Connect Web Form Fields to Template Fields
heading_path: Connect Web Form Fields to Template Fields
text:
Read about how template connections map Web Form fields to eSignature template fields so recipients enter information only once and see it everywhere it is used.

A template connection defines which data goes into a specific field by mapping a form field to a corresponding field in the imported eSignature template. Template fields must be mapped to form fields so recipients can see and use them.

Alternatively, you can connect fields in Document view by linking form fields directly to template fields in the document layout.

Most connections are automatically imported from the template to the form. You can manually connect a form field to a template field only when you add a new field to the form, and you can map multiple fields at once or individually.

To view all template field connections, go to the Signature page and select the Template Connections edit (pencil) icon. Or select More Actions (...) and Edit Template Connections.

![Template Connections page showing a list of template fields and their mapped web form fields, with options to edit or remove each connection.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gmi1660583110357/page/images/wf-manage-template-connections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTIxODksInNoZWFmIjoiZ21pMTY2MDU4MzExMDM1NyJ9.vVAhza3vti3XsGPsNl0I7HDVc8t1FetdzxOaxUeh8EI&_LANG=enus "Template Connections page showing a list of template fields and their mapped web form fields, with options to edit or remove each connection.")

You can map a form field to multiple template fields so the recipient enters the value only once and it displays everywhere that field is used in the template.

For example, map the form field "Last Name" to the template field "Last Name". If the field displays three times in the template, the recipient completes it once on the form, and the value displays in all three locations.

title: Run a Web Form Data Report
heading_path: Run a Web Form Data Report
text:
Learn how to use the Web Forms data report in Docusign to track field-level form responses, filter results by date, status, and other fields, and generate visual summaries that highlight key trends.

The Web Form data report is a Document Data report in Docusign that provides field-level

For example, a Real Estate agency uses a form to collect loan applications. Analysts use the Web Forms data report to track completed applications, review application volume over the last year, and identify trends. These insights help the Marketing team focus promotions and ad spending where they are most effective.

1. Go to the Insights page.
2. Select Reports > Web Forms.
3. Use the dropdown to select a form to analyze from your active forms.
4. Select a date range from the Date Range field.

   The available date ranges are:
   - Last 30 days: Previous 30 calendar days.
   - This Month: From the first day of the current month to 24 hours ago (default).
   - Last Month: First to last day of the previous calendar month.
   - Last 90 days: Previous 90 calendar days.
   - Last 3 Months: First to last day of the previous three calendar months.
   - Last 6 Months: First to last day of the previous six calendar months.
   - This Year: January 1 to 24 hours ago in the current year.
   - Last Year: January 1 to December 31 of the previous year.
   - Last 12 Months: First to last day of the previous 12 calendar months.
   - Custom: Select a custom date ranging from April 1, 2023 to 24 hours ago.
5. Select Run report.

   The report displays all form fields as columns, plus these columns:

   - Created On: The date the form response was created. A "response" occurs when users complete the form and create an envelope. They can then complete the envelope, setting the status to Completed, or abandon it, leaving the status as Sent.
   - Envelope Id: The envelope ID.
   - Status: The envelope status: Sent or Completed.
   - Completed On: The date when the form response envelope was signed and completed.
6. By default, all report columns display. To hide or rearrange columns, select Edit Columns, adjust the settings in the Edit Columns window, and select Done.
7. Apply filters to refine report data by setting conditions for specific form fields.
   1. Select Edit Filters > Add a condition, and choose the field, operator, and value for each condition. Available conditions depend on the selected field type.
   2. Select Save >  Run report.

   The report displays a list of the responses for your form that meet all the
   conditions you specified.
8. Analyze your data further by summarizing the data for each field type. You can
   display the data as a visual chart or a data table.
   1. Select the Summarize Results By dropdown and choose a form field to summarize.
   2. Select Run report.

      Your data summary displays as a bar or pie chart with a data table, depending on the field type. For bar charts, you can switch to a table view by selecting More Actions (...).
   3. For bar charts, you can switch to a table view by selecting More Actions (...).
9. Download the report data as a CSV file. After you run the report, select Download a report (down arrow) icon.

   Your data downloads as a CSV file to your local machine.
10. To reuse a report, select Save Report, enter a title and description, and select Save.

    Your custom report is saved and is displayed in the Reports list under Custom.

You have configured and run a Web Forms data report, applied filters, summarized results, downloaded the data, and optionally saved the report for future use.

title: The Web Forms Builder Properties and Rules Tab
heading_path: The Web Forms Builder Properties and Rules Tab
text:
Read about the Properties and Rules tabs in the Web Forms Builder, which allow configuration of form fields and control of component display based on recipient responses.

The Properties and Rules tabs display on the right side of the Web Forms Builder. Configure form fields in Properties or Rules and view the changes in the center panel.

![A field name being edited in the Properties tab of the Web Forms Builder.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gmi1660583110357/page/images/wf-properties-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTIxMTYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ21pMTY2MDU4MzExMDM1NyJ9.YUxJ8n-NbZUH1hrF8ngQkP9ACn2xb6ipSa5uN4niQ9Q&_LANG=enus "A field name being edited in the Properties tab of the Web Forms Builder.")

Each field type has unique properties. For information about the field properties, see the [Configure Web Form Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=awi1670975283111.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

You can also add rules to form fields to control component display based on recipient responses. For more information about field rules, see the [Add Rules to a Web Form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=kff1668121038223.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Transfer a Web Form Configuration File
heading_path: Transfer a Web Form Configuration File
text:
Learn how to transfer a Web Form configuration file to create a new form or move an existing form between environments or users.

You can download an existing form configuration and upload it to create a new form or transfer it between environments or users, including the same user on the same account.

1. Select My Web Forms, Shared with Me, or All Web Forms to choose the form that you want to transfer.
2. Select More Actions (...) > Download Configuration.

   The JSON configuration file downloads.
3. Upload the configuration file by selecting Start > Web Forms > Upload Web Form.

The form is created or updated based on the uploaded configuration file, making it available for use in the selected environment.

title: Delete a Web Form from Your Account
heading_path: Delete a Web Form from Your Account
text:
Learn how to delete a Web Form from your eSignature account.

You can delete a form from your eSignature account. After deletion, the form is removed from your Envelope Templates list and can no longer be used to send new documents.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select My Web Forms, Shared with Me, or All Web Forms and choose the form that you want to delete.

   Tip: If the form is open, select More Actions (...) > Delete Form.
3. Select More Actions (...) > Delete.

   The Confirm Form Delete window displays.
4. Select
   Confirm to delete the form.

The form is deleted from your account and removed from the Envelope Templates list.

title: Prepare a Template to Import to a Web Form
heading_path: Prepare a Template to Import to a Web Form
text:
Read about how to prepare an eSignature template so it meets all requirements before importing it into the Web Form Builder.

Before you create a form from a template, prepare the template, then copy and import it into the Web Form Builder, where it becomes a form template.

Note: The form template is a copy of the eSignature template that you imported into the Web Form Builder. In your eSignature instance, the Template tab shows only the eSignature template, while the Web Form Builder shows only the form template.

You can access the form template in the Web Form Builder anytime to review or make changes to a form. To view the template, go to the Signature page of the builder and select the Template Edit (pencil) icon.

To edit template settings and fields, select More Actions (...) at the top of the form and select Edit Template Settings and Edit Template Fields.

Each template field maps one-to-one to a form field, and you cannot change a field’s type after import. For example, you cannot import a text field from a template and then change it to a dropdown field in the form.

Before you import a template to the Web Form Builder, make sure that the template meets these requirements:

- The template must include at least one document.
- The primary recipient must have at least one text, checkbox, dropdown, or radio field. Otherwise, nothing is imported from the template to the form.

  Note: Other eSignature field types (such as drawing, formula, and attachments) still work in the eSignature template but are not imported into the form.
- Each recipient must have a unique role name. For example, do not assign the role name "Applicant" to two recipients. Instead, use "Applicant" and "Co-Applicant".
- The first recipient in the signing order must have the signing action Needs to Sign.
  - If you have only one recipient, that recipient must have the Needs to Sign signing action.
  - If you have multiple recipients, the first recipient must have the Needs to Sign signing action.

- Data labels must be non-blank and descriptive. A data label is a field identifier visible only to the sender. If a label is blank or has a non-descriptive identifier, update it to a more descriptive name. For more information about labels, see the [Create a Data Label on a Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=yis1665092718418.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how to create clear, descriptive data labels on template fields so senders can easily identify each form field.") guide.
- The template cannot include I, Witness, or Notary recipients.
- The template cannot include Salesforce, Healthcare, or other merged fields.
- Recipients must use email as the delivery option.

title: Create a Data Label on a Template
heading_path: Create a Data Label on a Template
text:
Read about how to create clear, descriptive data labels on template fields so senders can easily identify each form field.

Form fields inherit the template's data labels, which only the sender can see. When you add a field to a template, its data label displays in the Properties tab. By default, the label is a generic identifier that usually matches the field name with extra characters, such as "Text 1234".

Change this default identifier to a more descriptive name. In the Data Label field, enter a name such as "Co-Applicant Signature".

For example, a checkbox field might have the default identifier "Checkboxe88cf158-5c82-4892" in the Data Label field. Rename this label to something more descriptive, such as "Completed by Interviewer".

Text field data label
:   A text field lets recipients enter larger amounts of free-form text, such as Name, Address, or List of medications. Because a document can contain several text fields, give each one a unique name.

    - No label can be left blank.
    - Every label name must be descriptive. We strongly suggest using the template field name. For example, if the template field is "Applicant", you might name the data label "Applicant".

Dropdown field data label
:   A dropdown field lets recipients select a single option from a list you define.

    - No label can be left blank.
    - Every label must be unique.
    - Every label name must be descriptive. Docusign strongly suggests using the template field name.
    - Use only letters, numbers, and underscores. Spaces are not supported.
    - Start every label with a letter.

Checkbox field data label
:   This standard checkbox lets recipients select one or more options. You can add a single box or a group of boxes and change the data label for individual boxes or the entire group.

    - No label can be left blank.
    - Every label must be unique.
    - Every label must be descriptive. Docusign strongly suggests using the template field name. For example, if the template field is "Applicant", you might name the data label "Applicant".
    - If you create a checkbox group, you must add a descriptive Group Label name.
    - Use only letters, numbers, and underscores. Spaces are not supported.
    - Start every label with a letter.

Radio field data label
:   Radio fields let recipients select only one option from a group. You can add, remove, or reposition individual buttons.

    - No label can be left blank.
    - Every label must be unique.
    - Every label must be descriptive. We strongly suggest using the template field name.
    - If you create an option group, you must add a descriptive Group Label name.
    - Use only letters, numbers, and underscores. Spaces are not supported.
    - Start every label with a letter.

title: Build or Update a Web Form with Attachment Fields
heading_path: Build or Update a Web Form with Attachment Fields
text:
Read about how to build or update a Web Form with attachment fields or import them from a template that includes attachments.

You can create attachment fields directly on a form or import them from a template that includes them. After you import the attachment fields, recipients can upload documents only through the form, not through the template.

Note: The attachment feature is available for forms built with templates and [standalone forms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=pbx1716585514741.html&_LANG=enus). In standalone forms, attachment features are available only when the form is used in a Docusign Workflow Builder workflow. Your account must be enabled with Workflow Builder to see this option. For more information about using attachments in Docusign Workflow Builder, see the [Docusign Workflow Builder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yff1696971835267&topicId=dnx1696972415150.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

Recipients can upload only one file per attachment field, so anticipate how many attachments they may need. For example, if you import an attachment field for "Lease Agreements" and users might have multiple agreements, add additional attachment fields to the web form. You can add up to 15 attachment fields per form.

To import a template with attachment fields, build the form, and the fields are imported automatically. To add attachment fields manually, see the [Importing Attachment Fields from a eSignature Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=oyz1722621351928.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Generate a Web Forms Field-Level Data Report
heading_path: Generate a Web Forms Field-Level Data Report
text:
Read about how you can generate reports to view and analyze field-level data for the Web Forms you own.

Web Form reports give form owners detailed access to agreement data from form responses, helping you unlock data value and uncover insights.

The Web Forms report includes data about the envelope created from each form response, such as envelope ID, status, and creation and completion dates.

Report settings let you organize data by date range, status, and other form data points. You can also summarize field results to create visualizations that highlight insights, trends, and data-driven decisions.

Important: Form data can take 1–2 hours to become available for reporting or download.

For more information about this report and how to use it to access, analyze, and visualize your Web Forms data, see the [Run a Web Form Data Report](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=vsu1689289293172.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Web Forms data report in Docusign to track field-level form responses, filter results by date, status, and other fields, and generate visual summaries that highlight key trends.") guide.

title: Web Forms for FedRAMP Accounts
text:
Read about how FedRAMP account administrators can enable Web Forms for their users, similar to other accounts, without any special tasks.

Web Forms is available for government and FedRAMP plans. FedRAMP account administrators can enable Web Forms for users without any special tasks. Administrators and users can start creating or using forms immediately.

For more information about Docusign and FedRAMP, see the [FedRAMP - Docusign for Government](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/FedRamp-FAQ?language=en_US) article.

title: Add a Brand to a Web Form
heading_path: Add a Brand to a Web Form
text:
Read about how a Web Form inherits branding from its associated template, including logos and color schemes, affecting the appearance of elements.

A form inherits the signing branding from its associated template. If the imported template has a specific logo and color scheme, the form uses the same elements, which determine the appearance of the landing page.

You can choose a brand from the template by selecting Preview > Branding.

Brand information is stored on the Admin page. As an administrator, you can add and update the brands there. For more information about branding your form, see the [Add a Signing Brand for Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=byg1583277338658.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: The Web Forms Builder Center Panel
heading_path: The Web Forms Builder Center Panel
text:
Read about the center panel in the Web Forms Builder, which displays fields from the Outline and allows for field additions and deletions.

Fields listed in the Outline display in the center panel, originating from data labels in the imported template or standalone forms. Edits and rules you apply also display in the center panel.

You can add or delete fields in the center panel. Select the plus sign (+) to add a field, or the trash can icon to delete one.

For more information about adding fields or pages, see the [Configure Web Form Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gmi1660583110357&topicId=awi1670975283111.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

![Center panel of the Web Forms builder showing fields from the Outline and controls to add or delete fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gmi1660583110357/page/images/wf-center-panel.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTIxMTQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ21pMTY2MDU4MzExMDM1NyJ9.0r3lSYwKNnGDpNelxN2paM4tJmFpN9746_ehb2eIXiY&_LANG=enus "Center panel of the Web Forms builder showing fields from the Outline and controls to add or delete fields.")
